metadata.notes: Generated by Protocol Intelligence Platform. SR- IDs are placeholders.

general_information.notes: To be completed by Risk Manager upon IRMS entry

study_overview.title: TACTIVE-U: An Umbrella Study to Investigate the Safety and Antitumor Activity of ARV-471 in Combination With Other Medicines in Adults With ER+ Advanced or Metastatic Breast Cancer, Sub-study B (ARV-471 and Ribociclib)

critical_factors.critical_data: Dose-limiting toxicities (DLTs) in Cycle 1; Tumor response data (ORR, DoR, CBR, PFS) per RECIST v1.1; PK data (AUC, Cmax) for ARV-471 and Ribociclib
critical_factors.critical_process: Accurate and timely tumor assessments per RECIST v1.1; Precise collection of PK samples at protocol-specified timepoints for DDI evaluation

critical_data.signal: EP_SC_03_ORR - Objective response rate per RECIST v1.1

critical_process.signal: Evaluate the effect of ARV-471 on the pharmacokinetics of ribociclib

critical_factors.critical_data: AE/SAE data; DLTs during Cycle 1; ECG data (QTcF); Clinical laboratory data (hepatic, renal, hematologic)
critical_factors.critical_process: Timely reporting of DLTs to inform dose escalation/selection; Routine monitoring of ECGs for QT prolongation and labs for cytopenias/hepatic dysfunction

critical_data.signal: EP_SC_01_AE - Adverse events characterized by type, frequency, severity
EP_SC_07_INC_LAB_ECG - Incidence of laboratory abnormalities and ECG abnormalities

critical_factors.factor_name: Data Integrity and Reliability of Trial Results
critical_factors.critical_data: Concomitant medication records (especially CYP3A inhibitors/inducers); ctDNA and biomarker data
critical_factors.critical_process: Rigorous ConMed review by site to prevent use of prohibited CYP3A inhibitors/inducers and QT-prolonging drugs; Proper handling, processing, and shipment of PK and biomarker samples

critical_data.signal: Exclusion 10 - Concurrent administration of strong inhibitors/inducers of CYP3A
EP_EX_02_CT DNA - Quantitative changes in plasma ctDNA

critical_process.signal: Evaluate plasma exposure of ARV-471, ARV-473, and ribociclib

critical_factors.critical_data: Prior therapy history (exactly 1 prior CDK4/6 inhibitor); ER+/HER2- status; Baseline tumor scans; Baseline laboratory values
critical_factors.critical_process: Verification of prior lines of therapy and exact CDK4/6 inhibitor exposure; Confirmation of adequate washout periods for prior treatments and prohibited medications

critical_data.signal: Inclusion 2 - 1, and only 1, line of any prior CDK4/6 inhibitor-based regimen is required
Exclusion 11, 12, 13 - Hepatic, renal, and hematologic abnormalities

critical_process.signal: Exclusion 7 - Washout periods for prior anticancer treatments

critical_factors.critical_data: Dosing records for ARV-471 and Ribociclib; Dose modifications, interruptions, or discontinuations
critical_factors.critical_process: Managing complex dosing schedules (ARV-471 continuous vs Ribociclib 21 days on/7 days off); Ensuring correct dose levels are administered during Phase 1 dose escalation

critical_data.signal: Ribociclib 21 days on/7 days off per 28-day cycle

critical_process.signal: Starting dose level of ARV-471 100 mg QD continuous, Ribociclib 600 mg QD 21 days on/7 days off

risks.risk_description: Participant takes prohibited CYP3A inhibitors/inducers or QT-prolonging drugs, impacting safety or DDI endpoints.
risks.additional_context: Protocol strictly prohibits strong CYP3A inhibitors/inducers and QT-prolonging drugs (Exclusion 10). For the DDI Assessment Cohort, moderate CYP3A inhibitors/inducers are also prohibited to avoid confounding the PK analysis.

associated_causes.cause: Investigator prescribes a prohibited medication without checking the restricted list.

usdm_trigger.signal: Exclusion 10 - Concurrent administration of strong inhibitors and strong inducers of CYP3A

associated_causes.cause: Participant takes over-the-counter herbal supplements (e.g., St. John's Wort) that induce CYP3A.

usdm_trigger.signal: Exclusion 10 - herb supplements that are strong inhibitors and strong inducers

critical_factors_linked: Data Integrity and Reliability of Trial Results

controls.control_description: Implement eCRF edit checks to flag the entry of prohibited concomitant medications.
controls.additional_context: Focus specifically on CYP3A inhibitors/inducers and QT-prolonging agents.

controls.control_description: Routine medical review of concomitant medication logs.
controls.additional_context: Ensure immediate action if a prohibited medication is identified, especially in the DDI cohort.

intelligence.notes: Protocol strictly prohibits strong CYP3A inhibitors/inducers and QT-prolonging drugs (Exclusion 10). For the DDI Assessment Cohort, moderate CYP3A inhibitors/inducers are also prohibited to avoid conf

risks.risk_description: Enrolled participants do not meet the strict prior therapy requirements or organ function criteria.
risks.additional_context: Eligibility requires exactly 1 prior CDK4/6 inhibitor and specific washout periods. It also has strict cutoffs for hepatic, renal, and hematologic function, which may be challenging in an advanced/metastatic breast cancer population.

associated_causes.cause: Misinterpretation of prior lines of therapy (e.g., enrolling a patient with 2 prior CDK4/6 inhibitors).

usdm_trigger.signal: Inclusion 2 - 1, and only 1, line of any prior CDK4/6 inhibitor-based regimen

associated_causes.cause: Inadequate washout period for prior anticancer treatments.

usdm_trigger.signal: Exclusion 7 - Washout periods for prior therapies

controls.control_description: Medical monitor review of all screen failures and enrolled participants' prior therapy history.
controls.additional_context: Verify exact CDK4/6 inhibitor exposure prior to enrollment approval.

intelligence.notes: Eligibility requires exactly 1 prior CDK4/6 inhibitor and specific washout periods. It also has strict cutoffs for hepatic, renal, and hematologic function, which may be challenging in an advanced/met

risks.risk_description: PK, ctDNA, or tumor tissue samples are incorrectly obtained, processed, or shipped, impacting primary/secondary endpoints.
risks.additional_context: The study has critical PK objectives (DDI between ARV-471 and Ribociclib) and exploratory biomarker endpoints (ctDNA) requiring precise sample handling and timing.

associated_causes.cause: Site staff miss critical PK timepoints relative to dosing.
Improper processing or freezing of ctDNA samples leading to degradation.

critical_factors_linked: Data Integrity and Reliability of Trial Results

controls.control_description: Provide detailed laboratory manual and training on PK and biomarker sample collection, processing, and shipping.
controls.additional_context: Emphasize the importance of exact timing for DDI PK draws.

intelligence.notes: The study has critical PK objectives (DDI between ARV-471 and Ribociclib) and exploratory biomarker endpoints (ctDNA) requiring precise sample handling and timing.

risks.risk_description: Lack of appropriate or timely reporting of DLTs or SAEs during the combination treatment.
risks.additional_context: As a Phase I/II study evaluating a novel combination, accurate capture of DLTs in Cycle 1 and ongoing safety (ECGs, labs) is critical for dose selection and participant safety.

associated_causes.cause: Site fails to report a DLT within the required timeframe during Cycle 1.
Misgrading of adverse events per NCI CTCAE v5.0.

controls.control_type: Monitors reinforce requirements to report late SAEs within 24 hours (per INV-GSOP-SD-GL)
controls.control_description: CRAs to verify timely reporting of all SAEs and DLTs during monitoring visits.
controls.additional_context: Special focus on Cycle 1 DLT observation period.

intelligence.notes: As a Phase I/II study evaluating a novel combination, accurate capture of DLTs in Cycle 1 and ongoing safety (ECGs, labs) is critical for dose selection and participant safety.

risks.risk_description: Participant is given the wrong dose or administers the combination incorrectly due to differing schedules.
risks.additional_context: The regimen involves two oral drugs with different schedules: ARV-471 is continuous, while Ribociclib is 21 days on / 7 days off per 28-day cycle, increasing the risk of dosing errors at home.

associated_causes.cause: Participant confusion over the 21 days on / 7 days off schedule for Ribociclib.

usdm_trigger.signal: Ribociclib 600 mg QD - 21 days on/7 days off

associated_causes.cause: Incorrect dose level assigned or taken following a dose modification for toxicity.

usdm_trigger.signal: Multiple dose levels for Ribociclib (600mg, 400mg, 200mg) and ARV-471 (100mg, 200mg)

controls.control_description: Provide clear, participant-facing dosing diaries and instructions.
controls.additional_context: Visually distinguish the continuous ARV-471 schedule from the 21/7 Ribociclib schedule.

intelligence.notes: The regimen involves two oral drugs with different schedules: ARV-471 is continuous, while Ribociclib is 21 days on / 7 days off per 28-day cycle, increasing the risk of dosing errors at home.
